* Move tests to new server config

* Typo

* Get tracing middleware working

* Tidy up tracing now we're fully future based

* Tidy up server start

* Disable the tests that we know won't work at the moment

* All tests that should work currently running without hanging

* Wait for the port properly without needing to sleep. Disable hanging tests

* Enable report metrics flag

* Fix typo

* Fix cookie parsing

* Fix up some header tests

* Header tests working

* Tidy up BindAddress

* Clean up code

* Remove redundant warning

* Lots of tidy ups

* More tidy ups

* Update `File` multipart conformance

* Fix some Sendable stuff

* Bring back most of the client tests

* Tidy up existing tests

* Migrate testing application over to new helper

* Start exposing the new body and get all client tests working

* Don't allow chaining on .test() calls

* Rough out new testing API for multiple requests in a test

* Test bug annotations

* Get the new API working

* Fix up last route test with new API

* Fix FileMiddleware for Vapor 5

* Migrate the new tests for Vapor 5

* Bump to 6.1

* A big old pile of fixing up references in comments, removing spurious uses of `return`, cleaning up code style, adding missing uses of self., removing unneeded imports, and rewriting <E:Encodable>(_: E) as (some Encodable).

* AuthenticationCache no longer needs UnsafeAuthenticationBox.

* Adjust BasicCodingKey and its call sites for the fact that CodingKeyRepresentable can now be relied on.

* Fix missing Sendable requirement on JSONEn/Decoder userInfo.

* Use String(validating:as:) instead of using unsafe pointers.

* Instead of silencing all Concurrency warnings from RoutingKit, mark the TrieRouter in DefaultResponder as nonisolated(unsafe) (we use it safely in practice)

* Use Clock (via a trick borrowed from SwiftTesting) instead of Dispatch to get the uptime in nanoseconds

* Remove the weird random retroactive conformance of UUID to LosslessStringConvertible.

* URI and URI.Scheme should be ExpressibleByStringLiteral, not ExpressibleByStringInterpolation.

* Reminder

* Update NIO dependency and remove our equatable implementation

* Update gitignore

* Fix recording of elapsed time in metrics

* Junk a bunch of stuff in URI that compensates for cross-platform differences that no longer exist in Swift 6, and make it ExpressibleByStringInterpolation again

* Work around a Sendable issue with JSONEncoder's userInfo on Linux

* no point testing parallel if we're default anyway

* Turn on Android testing

* test: fix `system` in internal classes (#3339)

* fix! typo in `unexpected` - (Breaking change) (#3337)

* docs: fix `unexpected` results

* fix!: add the corrected wording for the error case

This change does not affect the library but it prevents any old switch case on the error.

* refactor: use the new corrected case

* feat: elevate the deprecated signature access level to public

* test: fix `configuration` argument (#3338)

* Fix compilation error

* Adopt service lifecycle

* Remove the service type

* Shutdown the thread pool correctly

* Remove the thread pool in favour of the singleton

* Simplyfy the password stuff and make it async by default

* Migrate the cache to new DI

* Fix the tests

* Improve the tests

* Migrate Client over to use DI

* Start migrating the tests

* Request tests passing

* Migrate the logger

* Get the tests passing

* Tidy up the tests

* Migrate away using future based AHC calls

* Sendable Metadata fixes

* Remove the old HTTP server

* More tidy up from the old HTTP server

* Remove AsyncKit since we don't use it

* Move bcrypt behind a package trait

* Use new router

* Fix compiler errors

* Fix merge issue

* Migrate to swift testing

* [Vapor 5] Migrate AHC, bcrypt, websockets and TLS to behind a package trait (#3356)

* Move websockets behind a trait

* Add @AuthMiddleware macro for type-safe authentication

Introduces @AuthMiddleware(Type.self, middleware...) that works with
HTTP method macros to extract authenticated users via req.auth.require()
and wrap route registration with .grouped() middleware in @Controller.
